text: Baek Cheon had no choice but to hang large question marks over his head as he watched Tang Soso.

speaker: narrator
text: Disliking the Southern Edge Sect was a must-have for those who wanted to become disciples of Mount Hua.

speaker: narrator
text: This wasn’t the fault of Mount Hua.

speaker: narrator
text: From the moment one entered Mount Hua, one would be beaten up by the Southern Edge Sect every two years.

speaker: narrator
text: ‘Soso.

speaker: narrator
text: You haven’t been on Mount Hua for even a year, thoUgh?’ Why was the youngest one feeling this bad?

speaker: narrator
text: Isn’t this an outburst for Mount Hua?

speaker: narrator
text: Baek Cheon looked at her with eyes filled with complex emotions.

speaker: narrator
text: For him, who still saw her as a flower-like woman, this version of her running up to the stage was still new.

speaker: narrator
text: Baek Cheon looked back at Yu Yiseol and asked, as she shook her head,

speaker: baek cheon
text: “Mount Hua’s sword… Soso hasn’t embodied it yet.”

speaker: baek cheon
text: “Winning isn’t the important thing. What matters is her will.”

speaker: narrator
text: ‘And it will be an experience.’ All Mount Hua wanted from Tang Soso and from the other Chung disciples wasn’t honor won from their spars.

speaker: narrator
text: A day would come to pass when Mount Hua became the center of it all, and they wanted her to learn and understand what she needed to do in order to gain power.

speaker: narrator
text: ‘Compared to what I hoped for, she is handling it several times better.’ Even so, with the exception of Yoon Jong and Jo Gul, she wasn’t inferior to the other Baek disciples.

speaker: narrator
text: Except for that idiot.

speaker: narrator
text: Baek Cheon looked at Tang Soso with worried eyes…

speaker: baek cheon
text: “Southern Edge Sect!”

speaker: narrator
text: Lee Song-Baek saw Tang Soso with alert eyes as she glared at him.

speaker: narrator
text: ‘What did I do wrong?’ Such knife-like eyes.

speaker: tang soso
text: “… I am Lee Song-Baek of the Southern Edge Sect. I will learn a few things.”

speaker: tang soso
text: “Mount Hua’s Tang Soso.”

speaker: narrator
text: Self-introductions were short, making Lee Song-Baek draw his sword with a bitter smile.

speaker: tang soso
text: “I have eyes, but my sword doesn’t, so please be careful not to get hurt.”

speaker: tang soso
text: “What do you mean? Be careful with your head, alright?”

speaker: narrator
text: Definitely a disciple from Mount Hua.

text: A shadow of Chung Myung was flickering behind Tang Soso.

text: Tang Soso frowned and glared at him.

speaker: narrator
text: ‘I will never lose to the Southern Edge Sect!’ Strangely, this man’s uniform was white, and this made Tang Soso angry.

speaker: narrator
text: This didn’t happen when she was in the Tang family, but seeing this happen after entering Mount Hua, it seems that there was a weird unknown relationship between the two.

speaker: narrator
text: She pulled the Plum Blossom Sword.

speaker: narrator
text: She hadn’t mastered the Mount Hua sword technique yet.

speaker: narrator
text: During her six months of training, her level of martial arts had risen, but it was impossible in the first place to catch up to her sahyungs, who had been learning it for more than ten years.

speaker: narrator
text: But just because she wasn’t skilled didn’t mean she would back down.

speaker: narrator
text: Lacking was lacking, and inability was inability.

speaker: narrator
text: But still, one had to go down their own path.

speaker: narrator
text: That was the teaching of Mount Hua’s Tang Soso, and that was her will.

speaker: narrator
text: Tang Soso, who was staring at Lee Song-Baek with cold eyes, rushed in without delay, The sight of her charging him was scary.

speaker: narrator
text: A destructive blow came out from her slender arms!

speaker: narrator
text: What she lacked was technique, not power.

speaker: narrator
text: As the daUghter of the Tang family, she had been training steadily to the extent that she was second to none.

speaker: narrator
text: It was no exaggeration to say that her strength was actually good even in Mount Hua, and the pill had helped her more.

speaker: narrator
text: The sword, flooded with qi, rushed for Lee Song-Baek.

speaker: narrator
text: Lee Song-Baek took two steps back lightly.

speaker: narrator
text: Tang Soso’s sword slammed into the stage, right where he had disappeared from, creating a massive explosion.

speaker: narrator
text: Lee Song-Baek was wide-eyed as he saw the deeply dented ground.

speaker: narrator
text: He was bewildered by that power.

text: “… you think I would get hit?”

speaker: narrator
text: Lee Song-Baek laUghed.

speaker: narrator
text: Certainly, the disciples of Mount Hua have a unique side.

speaker: narrator
text: ‘If this was in the past, then this would have been a bad look.’ Rather than discussing and gaining face outside and being poor on the inside, he had the true attitude of a warrior who was faithful to the basics.

speaker: narrator
text: Lee Song-Baek glanced away and looked at Chung Myung.

speaker: narrator
text: ‘Look carefully, Disciple Chung Myung.’ Whether my last two years have been wrong or not.

speaker: narrator
text: Did I go on the right path, as you said?

speaker: narrator
text: This was a spar.

speaker: narrator
text: But this wasn’t just a simple spar either.

speaker: narrator
text: For Lee Song-Baek, this was a place to prove his efforts to Chung Myung.

speaker: narrator
text: Lee Song-Baek raised his sword and aimed it at the center of the ring.

speaker: narrator
text: The basic form of the sword.

speaker: narrator
text: His breathing slowed.

speaker: narrator
text: The surrounding air began to turn heavy.

speaker: narrator
text: A simple sword with a stable center of gravity makes it ready for any situation.

speaker: narrator
text: Something that everyone knew but didn’t know to keep it.

speaker: narrator
text: The world calls it ‘the basic method to produce the best results.’ Lee Song-Baek’s sword, which received the sword of Tang Soso, collided with a terrifying force.

speaker: narrator
text: Never strong, but not weak either.

speaker: narrator
text: A sword that guarded the center.

speaker: narrator
text: Tang Soso’s face stiffened.

speaker: narrator
text: This was a sword that had nothing special on the outside.

speaker: narrator
text: It wasn’t flashy like the other Southern Edge techniques.

speaker: narrator
text: Nevertheless, this sword seemed different from what she had seen till now.

speaker: narrator
text: Tang Soso kicked the ground and widened the distance between them at once.

speaker: narrator
text: His serious eyes followed her.

speaker: narrator
text: A low groan flowed from her mouth, and Baek Cheon smiled.

speaker: narrator
text: ‘Lee Song-Baek was it?’ Obviously, there was nothing special about this guy in the past.

speaker: narrator
text: No, actually, even now, he didn’t seem special on the outside.

speaker: narrator
text: He didn’t feel as sharp as Jin Geum-Ryong and didn’t have the same spirit as the other disciples of the Southern Edge Sect.

speaker: narrator
text: From the outside, it looked as if he was soaked in calmness and drunk on alcohol.

speaker: narrator
text: Like a test waiting to be resolved.

speaker: narrator
text: Baek Cheon turned his head in surprise.

speaker: narrator
text: Chung Myung, who had returned to his seat, took some jerky out and bit into it.

speaker: chung myung
text: “… what are you saying?”

speaker: chung myung
text: “Literally, the basics.”

speaker: narrator
text: Chung Myung looked at Lee Song-Baek with a subtle smile.

speaker: chung myung
text: “What do you think a sword technique is?”

speaker: chung myung
text: “… a way to use the sword?”

speaker: chung myung
text: “Right, how to use the sword. How to use the different forms of a sword. But techniques come from three things.”

speaker: narrator
text: Chung Myung showed three fingers.

speaker: chung myung
text: “Poke. Swing and Block.”

speaker: chung myung
text: “In the end, all techniques are a mixture of these three. That’s what swords are like in the first place. There is nothing too complicated about it.”

speaker: chung myung
text: “Isn’t that overly simplistic?”

speaker: chung myung
text: “Those simple things add up to become complicated things. And in other words…”

speaker: narrator
text: Chung Myung sighed and continued,

speaker: chung myung
text: “If you can stab, swing, and block perfectly, then your sword will be perfect.”

speaker: chung myung
text: “Right. It is almost impossible. Perfection is a concept that does not exist. Obsessing over a perfect sword is something that only a madman would do. But…”

speaker: narrator
text: Chung Myung continued,

speaker: chung myung
text: “…the world and Shaolin. Something that the Wudang Sect, which tried to do something great, would never even dare to attempt. The world is made up of thirty-six directions, with me in the center. A madman believes that he can become the best swordsman out there if he can just stab, swing, and block the sword perfectly in all thirty-six directions around him.”

speaker: chung myung
text: “Right. Right,”

speaker: narrator
text: said Chung Myung softly, looking at Lee Song-Bawk.

speaker: chung myung
text: “The Heavenly Thirty-Six Stroke Sword technique.”

speaker: narrator
text: His heart didn’t flinch at all.

text: Even the sensation of the air passing throUgh his fingers could be clearly felt.

speaker: narrator
text: Lee Song-Baek could see that he was in his best form today.

speaker: narrator
text: It was several times more difficult to keep a steady form that wasn’t too forceful or too weak.

speaker: narrator
text: Southern Edge’s sword was a sword that guarded the center.

speaker: narrator
text: Not strong, not flashy, not fast.

speaker: narrator
text: Yet, this sword technique of the Southern Edge Sect was closer to being perfect than any other sword.

speaker: narrator
text: ‘If I had known this right away…’ So much time wouldn’t have been wasted.

speaker: narrator
text: But that was fine.

speaker: narrator
text: The path he would take had just been started.

speaker: narrator
text: Do not be deceived by the splendor and strength.

speaker: narrator
text: What he had to protect was the center.

speaker: narrator
text: This was the soul of the Southern Edge Sect.

speaker: narrator
text: Tang Soso looked at the guy with a stiff face.

speaker: narrator
text: ‘This man, what is he?’ She could feel a sense of weight that she had never felt from anyone other than Baek Cheon or Chung Myung.

speaker: narrator
text: Of course, it was nowhere near Chung Myung.

speaker: narrator
text: So he was a human, thus could never give off such a feeling… but to feel what she felt from Baek Cheon’s sword made her quite shocked.

speaker: narrator
text: ‘What are you doing, Tang Soso!’ Tang Soso was a bit shocked.

speaker: narrator
text: She knew that her opponent might be stronger than her.

speaker: narrator
text: Tang Soso raised her sword and aimed it at Lee Song-Baek.

speaker: narrator
text: No words were needed.

speaker: narrator
text: ‘I will paint it with my plum blossoms.’ There was nothing to be afraid of as long as she used her technique perfectly.

speaker: narrator
text: The two swordsmen looked at each other.

speaker: narrator
text: As if the tension was contagious, even the spectators were holding their breath.

speaker: narrator
text: Tang Soso was the first to move again, With a short cheer, she rushed in again.

speaker: narrator
text: Her sword cut throUgh the air, and it fell on Lee Song-Baek.

speaker: narrator
text: The plum blossoms she was trying to make were different from those of the other disciples from Mount Hua.

speaker: narrator
text: If the plum blossoms of Mount Hua were spring, then her plum blossoms were literally raindrops.

speaker: narrator
text: She was a disciple of Mount Hua but a daUghter of the Tang family.

speaker: narrator
text: Her talent, which didn’t blossom in the Tang family, became a sword of Mount Hua and rained flowers all over the world.

speaker: narrator
text: AlthoUgh it was Mount Hua’s sword, the plum blossoms were unique to Tang Soso, and her petals were blown by the gust of wind and covered Lee Song-Baek’s body.

speaker: narrator
text: And Lee Song-Baek’s eyes, seeing this scene, looked at her.

speaker: narrator
text: The most beautiful and sharp blossoms he had seen.

speaker: narrator
text: It seemed impossible to stop them all.

speaker: narrator
text: But Lee Song-Baek swung his sword without haste.

speaker: narrator
text: His legs were firm, shoulders gentle, elbows raised sharply like a whip, and his wrist ready to take the shock.

speaker: narrator
text: Swing, stab, and block.

speaker: narrator
text: Even if the plum blossoms of Mount Hua covered the world.

speaker: narrator
text: He had the Thirty-Six Stroke Movement.

speaker: narrator
text: If he could completely shake off the sword with the Thirty-Six Stroke Movement, then the rain of plum blossoms that filled this place could not touch his body.

speaker: narrator
text: Southern Edge’s sword was a sword of balance.

speaker: narrator
text: The basis of the sword was to block the opponent’s attack.

speaker: narrator
text: The most faithful to the basics of the sword was the sword of the Southern Edge Sect.

speaker: narrator
text: The plum blossoms fell down like a dream, losing their power in the face of Lee Song-Baek’s sword technique.

speaker: narrator
text: After flying like that, they crashed into his sword violently and then fell softly.

speaker: narrator
text: Tang Soso seemed shocked.

speaker: narrator
text: But she couldn’t give up.

speaker: narrator
text: She tried to do it again.

speaker: narrator
text: Lee Song-Baek’s sword, which whistled with the sound of shattering air, fell toward the head of Tang Soso.

speaker: narrator
text: Lee Song-Baek’s sword fell right on her shoulder.

speaker: narrator
text: Tang Soso bit her lip,

speaker: tang soso
text: “It was a good match.”

speaker: narrator
text: After Lee Song-Baek retrieved his sword, he bowed politely.

speaker: tang soso
text: “It was the sharpest and cleanest sword. If it was a little deeper, I would have been defeated.”

speaker: tang soso
text: “… I admit the winner’s words, but it would be better not to be overconfident. I will be the one who wins next time.”

speaker: tang soso
text: “Of course, I look forward to it.”

speaker: narrator
text: Seeing Lee Song-Baek’s selfless smiling face, Tang Soso let out a sigh.

speaker: narrator
text: She had no regrets about the fight that she just had.

speaker: narrator
text: And this defeat would only make Tang Soso stronger.

speaker: tang soso
text: “Don’t be elated about doing this. The sahyungs of mine are stronger.”

speaker: narrator
text: Lee Song-Baek sighed a little and turned his head to look at one place.

speaker: tang soso
text: “I know it better than anyone. A lot better.”

speaker: narrator
text: The place where his eyes, a mixture of envy and fighting spirit, touched Chung Myung’s.

speaker: chung myung
text: “Because it is also my goal.”

speaker: narrator
text: Lee Song-Baek’s eyes were full of light.

speaker: narrator
text: And after receiving his gaze, Chung Myung mumbled softly,

speaker: chung myung
text: “The end is another beginning.”

speaker: narrator
text: And that was right here.

speaker: narrator
text: A new seed was growing from the ashes.
